_meta.author: Claude (scorecard-refine-local autopilot · 2026-06-05 23:36 EDT)
_meta.note: Multi-state LOCAL batch: Bianco (CA, state-tier dual-office sheriff/gov), Stewart (CT, state-tier dual-office mayor/gov · SUSPENDED), Chapin (Orlando FL D3), Connors (Titusville FL Mayor), Nelson (FL 4th JC State Attorney). Heavy currency-check findings: Bianco LOST June 2 2026 jungle primary (4th, ~11%); Stewart SUSPENDED May 14 2026 amid criminal probe + did not seek mayoral reelection Nov 2025; Stuart (Orlando D3 predecessor) RETIRED not deceased. Evidence-only, social-media protocol, sources cited.

set.office: Sheriff of Riverside County, California (sitting · 2026 CA Governor primary candidate · LOST June 2 2026 jungle primary, 4th place ~11%)

profile.background: 14th Sheriff-Coroner of Riverside County, CA (since 2019, reelected 2022, term to 2028). Announced GOP gubernatorial run Feb 17, 2025; finished 4th in June 2 2026 jungle primary (~11%); Hilton (R) + Becerra (D) advance to November. Former Oath Keepers member 2014–15. Married to Denise; four children.

chad-bianco-gov.notes_set: [2026-06-05 evidence finalization · LOCAL autopilot · state-tier dual-office] Sitting Riverside Co Sheriff who ran for CA Gov 2026; LOST jungle primary June 2 2026 (4th, ~11%), eliminated from Nov general; remains Sheriff (term 2028). High-profile MAGA-aligned constitutional-sheriff: openly refused to enforce Newsom COVID lockdowns + vaccine mandates (Fox LA, Spectrum News 1); pro-life w/ explicit Comstock Act enforcement pledge (factually.co); pledged to abolish CA income tax (KTLA); pledged to abolish sanctuary state + repeal AB 1955 parental-notification override (campaign site); seized 650K ballots in 2025 special-election integrity action (CalMatters); affirms 2A as 'God-given right' (Survival World). Notable nuance scored FALSE on border q1: platform explicitly only deports 'violent criminals' + supports 'reasonable path to citizenship' + 'guest worker programs' (biancoforgovernor.com/immigration) — NOT a mandatory-deportation-of-all-illegals posture. Christian rhetoric strong but no Rom 13:4 magistrate-bearing-the-sword framing documented → q4 null.

0.note: Pro-life position affirming life begins at conception.

2.note: Pledged as governor would not fund Planned Parenthood.

4.note: Supports enforcement of Comstock Act banning interstate transport of chemical-abortion drugs.

2.note: 'Boys should not be competing against girls... ethically and morally wrong to allow them into locker rooms and restrooms.'

4.note: Pledged to repeal AB 1955 (CA law restricting parental notification of student gender changes).

1.note: Top priority: change CA policies 'that prohibit parental notification when students indicate they may be transgender'; repeal AB 1955.

2.note: Pledged repeal of AB 1955 and restoration of parental rights in schools.

0.note: 'Number one, I am a Christian'; 'Our Christian values absolutely must be in our government.'

3.note: 'Our government was formed only for a Christian nation. Without Christian values, our form of government will not work.'

0.note: Pledged to abolish the CA state income tax entirely.

3.note: April 2026 seized ~650,000 ballots from CA's 2025 special election citing integrity concerns; ongoing investigation.

0.note: Career sheriff running on 'law and order'; supported Prop 36; pledges full LE funding and reopening prisons.

1.note: Platform rolls back Prop 47 felony threshold; restores cash bail for serious/violent offenses; vocally criticizes 'zero-bail' and 'DA non-prosecution'.

0.note: Riverside County 'won't be blackmailed, bullied, or used as muscle' by state on COVID orders; asserted constitutional limits.

2.note: Publicly refused to enforce Newsom COVID lockdowns and vaccine mandate.

4.note: Refused to enforce COVID stay-at-home and vaccine-mandate orders deemed unconstitutional.

1.note: Platform supports deportation only of 'violent criminals'/'serious crimes' + 'reasonable path to citizenship for hard working, law abiding residents' + 'guest worker programs' — not a mandatory-deportation-of-all posture.

2.note: Pledges to 'abolish Sanctuary State policies'; attended Feb 21 2025 presser backing SB 554 to weaken CA sanctuary law.

4.note: Calls 2A / self-defense a 'God-given right' and check against government tyranny.

set.office: Former Mayor of New Britain, Connecticut (2013–2025 · did not seek 6th reelection Nov 2025 · 2026 R Governor candidate SUSPENDED May 14 2026 amid criminal probe)

profile.background: Erin Stewart (b. 1987), 40th Mayor of New Britain CT 2013–2025 (six terms, longest-serving R mayor in city history); Catholic; self-described 'fiscal conservative, social liberal' Republican; ran briefly for Gov 2018 then LG; declared for Gov Nov 2025; suspended May 14 2026 amid Crumbie Law Group investigative report alleging ~$207K personal/political charges on city credit card 2016–2025. CT State Police + CT AG opened separate probes.

erin-stewart-gov.notes_set: [2026-06-05 evidence finalization · LOCAL autopilot · state-tier dual-office] CURRENCY: did NOT seek reelection as New Britain Mayor Nov 2025; SUSPENDED gov campaign May 14 2026 amid criminal investigation (Crumbie Law Group fraud report → CT State Police + CT AG probes incl. Mayor's Trophy charitable fund subpoena June 2026 · CT Mirror). Textbook moderate / social-liberal Republican record. Pro-choice: kicked off gov campaign emphasizing pro-choice position; refused to run with a 'right winger' pro-lifer; cast deciding vote to allow Planned Parenthood to provide contraception in New Britain public schools (Family Institute of CT). Supports same-sex marriage; personally attended/spoke at New Britain Central Park Pride flag-raising and helped organize first NB Pride 2018; 'Pride in the City' month resolution under her tenure (CT Mirror, NB Herald, NB Independent). Gun controls: 'comfortable with reasonable controls'; declined to join Rs wanting to repeal CT post-Newtown gun laws. Explicitly refused to reinstate capital punishment (Rom 13:4 framing FALSE). One TRUE: balanced-budget posture as mayor (no-tax-increase budgets, reduced city headcount). DEVIATION ALERT: documented multi-source social-liberal R record — high-value catch. Record marked status=former (no longer holds office, withdrew from gov race).

0.note: Publicly pro-choice; kicked off gov campaign emphasizing pro-choice position; refused to run with a 'right winger' pro-lifer.

2.note: Cast deciding vote to allow Planned Parenthood to provide contraception in New Britain public schools (public-funded provision).

3.note: Pro-choice; no support for heartbeat or total ban.

0.note: Self-described supportive of gay rights; supports same-sex marriage.

4.note: Personally attended/spoke at New Britain Central Park Pride flag-raising; helped organize first NB Pride 2018; 'Pride in the City' month resolution under her admin.

1.note: Voted to allow Planned Parenthood to provide contraception in NB public schools without affirmative parental-consent framing; Family Institute of CT questioned her, she did not respond affirmatively.

2.note: As mayor proposed multiple no-tax-increase budgets and reduced city headcount; balanced-budget posture core to her fiscal-conservative brand.

4.note: Explicitly refused to support reinstating capital punishment; no Rom 13:4 magistrate-sword framing.

1.note: 'Comfortable with reasonable controls'; explicitly declined to join Republicans wanting to repeal CT post-Newtown gun controls (mag limits, AWB, registry).

set.office: City Commissioner, District 3, City of Orlando (sworn in Jan 12 2026 · 4-yr term to 2029)

profile.background: Lifelong Orlando resident; son of former Orange County Mayor Linda Chapin; 20+ years at Mears Transportation rising to EVP, later PR consultant (Chapin Communications); served on Municipal Planning Board, Downtown Development Board, OUC, Orlando Health Foundation; won Dec 9 2025 District 3 runoff 51.3% over Mira Tanna; endorsed by Mayor Buddy Dyer, Sheriff John Mina, FOP Lodge 25, Firefighters Local 1365, Florida Democrats; self-described 'govern from the middle' Democrat in nonpartisan race.

roger-chapin.notes_set: [2026-06-05 evidence finalization · LOCAL autopilot] CORRECTION to prior bio: Robert F. Stuart RETIRED in June 2025 (alive, endorsed Chapin) — NOT deceased. Chapin sworn in Jan 12 2026; ~5 months of voting record at this writing. Endorsed by Florida Democratic Party, FOP Lodge 25, Firefighters Local 1365, Sheriff Mina. Campaign platform explicitly: 'Roger will support law enforcement... ensure we have the resources we need to fight crime and keep our families safe' — meets HIGH-BAR affirmative-advocacy threshold for public_justice q0. On-record FALSE on immigration: stated to Orlando Weekly any ICE detention facility coming before Council would be approached 'with deep skepticism'; serves on Council the city attorney described as 'unequivocally' against an ICE facility (April 2026 WUSF). Otherwise silent on culture-war specifics — 27 cells null. Religion not publicly disclosed.

0.note: Endorsed by FOP Lodge 25, Firefighters Local 1365, Sheriff Mina; platform: 'Roger will support law enforcement... ensure we have the resources we need to fight crime and keep our families safe' — affirmative advocacy, not routine budget.

1.note: Publicly stated any ICE detention facility coming before Council would be approached 'with deep skepticism' — opposes federal immigration-enforcement infrastructure.

2.note: Sits on Council the city attorney described as 'unequivocally' against an ICE facility (April 2026); City of Orlando operates under Trust Act sanctuary-style policy and Chapin has not broken with that posture.

set.office: Mayor, City of Titusville, Florida (elected Nov 5 2024 · 4-yr term to 2028)

profile.background: Andrew Connors — BGSU grad and licensed nursing home administrator, President of Atlantic Enrollment Solutions; former Titusville YMCA Board Chair and Chamber of Commerce board member; married to Kelsey, two sons; organized the North Brevard Prayer Breakfast; elected Mayor Nov 5 2024 (defeated Democrat Vickie Conklin by ~400 votes); 4-year term to 2028.

andrew-connors.notes_set: [2026-06-05 evidence finalization · LOCAL autopilot] Confirmed sitting Republican Mayor of Titusville (Brevard County FL · Space Coast), term Nov 2024 – 2028. Public footprint thin on rubric culture-war issues — centers on housing/infrastructure/water-quality, Feb 2025 officer-involved-shooting community statement (transparency-framed, not theological), and a Mayor's Ball spending controversy. Affirmative LE-honoring action at National Night Out (City of Titusville official video) meets HIGH-BAR public_justice q0. Mayor's Ball spending flap (used ~$400 city resources without prior council approval for private fundraiser, then secured 4-1 council vote for up to $750 more in ticket spending) = FALSE on economic_stewardship q2 (poor stewardship of taxpayer funds). 28 of 30 cells = null — first-year small-town mayor with no culture-war record.

0.note: Affirmatively honored law-enforcement officers at National Night Out event (City of Titusville official video) — meets affirmative-advocacy bar.

2.note: Mayor's Ball controversy: used ~$400 of city resources without prior council approval for a private fundraiser; then secured 4-1 council vote for up to $750 more in ticket spending — conduct opposite of careful stewardship.

set.office: State Attorney, 4th Judicial Circuit of Florida (Duval/Clay/Nassau · sitting · unopposed 2024 reelection · 3rd term to Jan 2029)

profile.background: Republican; UF undergrad (1994 honors) and UF Levin College of Law JD (1997 honors); 12 years as Assistant State Attorney before private practice; elected State Attorney Nov 2016 defeating incumbent Angela Corey in GOP primary; took office Jan 3 2017; filed for third term April 5 2024 and qualified unopposed; reelected without appearing on ballot; current term Jan 2025 – Jan 2029; married to Jason Nelson, three children.

melissa-nelson.notes_set: [2026-06-05 evidence finalization · LOCAL autopilot] Career professional prosecutor; defeated Angela Corey in 2016 R primary; unopposed 2024 reelection. Strong public_justice signals — DOJ recognized her for Project Safe Neighborhoods LE partnership (TRUE q0); built Targeted Prosecution division for dangerous offenders + Crime Gun Intelligence Center, NOT a Soros-funded blanket-non-prosecution progressive (TRUE q1); robust Victim Assistance Program serving ~6,700 victims/yr (TRUE q2). One FALSE: publicly endorsed Cure Violence Chicago-model program which frames violence as a 'public health' problem (News4Jax 2019) → public_justice q3 FALSE. Added extra review layer before pursuing death penalty (procedural caution, not theological refusal) → q4 null. Effectively SILENT on culture-war cells (abortion enforcement post-Dobbs, marriage, family/parental rights, election fraud, immigration cooperation, 2A principle) — 22+ cells null. Profile: technocratic reform-conservative, victim-focused, not a movement Christian-conservative.

0.note: DOJ recognized her for Project Safe Neighborhoods LE partnership; cleared officers in 14-yr-old shooting + violent traffic stop — affirmative pro-LE record.

1.note: Career prosecutor; unseated Angela Corey in 2016 GOP primary as professional reformer (NOT Soros-funded progressive); built Targeted Prosecution division for dangerous offenders + Crime Gun Intelligence Center; no blanket non-prosecution policies.

2.note: Robust Victim Assistance Program; team of victim specialists assisted ~6,700 individuals in one year; office provides restitution/compensation navigation.

3.note: Publicly endorsed Cure Violence program which frames violence as a public-health problem; joined Mayor Curry + Sheriff Williams supporting Chicago-model intervention in Jacksonville.
